rulings.Case Title: SAEED FAZELL VS. JULIE A MARGETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kasolas - Petition To Cancel Sale - Notice Of Motion - Signed
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 21, 2021 line 1. REFEREE MICHAEL KASOLAS' PETITION TO CANCEL SALE is DENIED without prejudice. Referee to take steps towards delivery of property free of occupants and may re-file this application if escrow is not closed thereafter. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC. VS. PAZZIA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 21, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of material fact 3, 12, 21, 27. The Court further notes that it is unclear from the Notice of Motion whether Plaintiff is seeking Summary Judgment on both the Complaint and Cross-Complaint, while the alternative relief for Summary Adjudication clearly covers both. Summary Judgment motion is framed by the pleadings and the Court cannot adjudicate partial causes of action (except for issues that are not applicable here). The first cause of action in the Cross-Complaint is based, inter alia, on the following allegations that are not addressed by the moving party: "[Landlord] has further breached the contract and the covenant of good faith and fair dealing by agreeing to accept a percentage of the rent and thereafter reneging on the amount it was willing to accept. It has further breached the covenant of good faith and fair dealing by refusing to negotiate payments with Pazzia unless Pazzia paid it additional amounts that Pazzia did not agree to pay after the onset of the pandemic and the closure ordered by the Governor of California." Therefore, moving party failed to shift its burden on the first cause of action in the Cross-Complaint both for the purposed of summary judgment (assuming summary judgment was sought on the cross-complaint) and on the summary adjudication. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
